subject: Lease renewal packet — review ahead of October renewal
snippet: Hi Karen — the Coral Ridge Professional Center lease renewal packet arrived early. The renewal term begins October 1...
body:
Hi Karen,

The lease renewal packet from Coral Ridge Professional Center arrived today. The renewal term begins October 1; the landlord wants signed intent well ahead of that date or we roll month-to-month at a 12% premium, so they sent it early this year.

I've flagged three items for your review:
1. Rent escalation clause (Sec. 4.2) — 3% annual, same as current
2. CAM cap increased from 8% to 10%
3. Option to renew for an additional 3 years added at our request

I can drop the packet on your desk this afternoon. Should I also loop in Anthony Beaumont to review the financial terms before you sign?

Danielle

subject: Re: Lease renewal packet — review ahead of October renewal
snippet: Danielle — yes, please loop in Anthony. I'll review this week...
body:
Danielle,

Thank you for flagging this promptly. Yes, please loop in Anthony — copying him here. Anthony, can you review Sec. 4.2 and the CAM cap change and let me know if there are any concerns by the end of next week?

Danielle, go ahead and leave the packet on my desk. I'll review this week, and we'll target signing well ahead of the October renewal.

Karen

subject: Dec 5 NAMI FL Panel — logistics and AV needs
snippet: Karen — you're confirmed for the Dec 5 panel. A few logistics details before we send the final confirmation...
body:
Karen,

You're confirmed for the December 5 NAMI Florida panel at the Miami Beach Convention Center. A few logistics items before we send the final confirmation to the venue:

1. Panel title: "Mental Health Equity in South Florida: Gaps, Gains, and the Road Ahead" — still works for you?
2. Your 20-minute segment is slotted 2:15–2:35 PM. Full panel Q&A runs 2:35–3:15 PM.
3. AV: Do you need a clicker/laser pointer or will slides be pre-loaded on the house laptop?
4. Hotel block at the Loews Miami Beach closes Nov 14. Would you like a room reserved on the event account?
5. Honorarium paperwork — please send your W-9 to this address at your convenience.

Let us know on the above and we'll get everything finalized.

NAMI Florida Events

subject: Re: Dec 5 NAMI FL Panel — logistics and AV needs
snippet: Panel title works perfectly. Here are my answers to your logistics questions...
body:
Thank you. Panel title works perfectly. Here are my answers:

1. Title confirmed.
2. 2:15–2:35 PM slot confirmed — I'll plan to be on-site by 1:30 PM.
3. Please pre-load slides on the house laptop. I'll send the deck (16:9 PPTX) by Nov 28. I do have my own clicker if needed.
4. Yes, please reserve a room on the event account for the night of Dec 4. One king, non-smoking.
5. W-9 attached — let me know if you need anything else from me.

Looking forward to it.

Karen Bell, PMHNP-BC
Bell Behavioral Health
(954) 555-0100

subject: Fall 2026 midterm eval deadline — Oct 15
snippet: Karen — just a reminder that midterm student evaluations for Cultural Competence in Psychiatric Practice are due by Oct 15...
body:
Hi Karen,

Hope the semester is going well. Just a reminder that midterm evaluations for your section of Cultural Competence in Psychiatric Practice (PMHNP 612) are due by October 15 in the NSU faculty portal.

Please complete a brief narrative eval for each enrolled student covering: engagement and participation, clinical application of course concepts, and any areas for development.

If you run into any portal access issues, contact IT at nova-it-help@nova.edu. Let me know if you have questions about the evaluation rubric.

Best,
Nadine Cherry, PhD
Adjunct Faculty Coordinator, NSU PMHNP Program

subject: Re: Fall 2026 midterm eval deadline — Oct 15
snippet: Nadine — thanks for the reminder. I have portal access and will have all evals submitted by Oct 14...
body:
Nadine,

Thank you for the reminder. I have portal access and will have all evaluations submitted by October 14 — one day ahead of the deadline.

Quick question: for students who have missed more than one class session, should I note attendance in the narrative or flag it separately through the dean's office process?

Karen

subject: Q3 QuickBooks reconciliation — items for your review
snippet: Karen — completed the Q3 reconciliation in QuickBooks. Three items need your sign-off before I close the quarter...
body:
Karen,

Completed the Q3 reconciliation in QuickBooks. Overall the quarter looks clean. Three items need your attention before I close the period:

1. Two credit card charges in September ($340 total) are categorized as "Meals & Entertainment" but the receipts haven't been uploaded. Can you locate and upload those?
2. The continuing education reimbursement for Dr. Foster — I need confirmation this should be booked as a payroll benefit vs. a direct expense.
3. The deposit from the NSU adjunct stipend (Q3 installment) needs to be confirmed as personal vs. practice income for allocation purposes.

Please respond by Oct 20 so I can close Q3 and begin the Q4 setup.

Anthony Beaumont, CPA

subject: Claire's birthday dinner — Nov 15 plans
snippet: Hey — have you thought about where you want to take Claire for her birthday dinner? She mentioned Louie Bossi or Steak 954...
body:
Hey Karen,

Have you thought about where you want to take Claire for her birthday dinner on the 15th? She dropped a hint last week — mentioned Louie Bossi or Steak 954. Oliver said he can drive down from Boca that Saturday and would be back Sunday.

Should we make it a surprise dinner or let her pick? And do you want to see if Philip can drive over from Orlando, or keep it just the four of us this year?

Also — cake from Rosarians or do you want to order from that bakery on Federal again?

John

subject: Re: Claire's birthday dinner — Nov 15 plans
snippet: Let's do Steak 954 — Claire has been talking about it since summer. I'll make a reservation for 7 PM...
body:
Let's do Steak 954 — Claire has been talking about it since the summer. I'll call for a 7 PM reservation this week and put it under our name. Oliver, glad you can make it — just text me your ETA Saturday afternoon.

Let's keep it a surprise and tell her we're just going to dinner. Keep it immediate family only this year — I'll ask Philip if he wants to drive down Sunday for the dessert get-together at the house.

I'll order the cake from the bakery on Federal (the salted caramel one she loved last year). Will pick it up Saturday afternoon.

Karen

subject: Oct 22 staff meeting — agenda items
snippet: Hi Karen — ahead of Thursday's staff meeting I wanted to flag a few items I'd like to add to the agenda...
body:
Hi Karen,

Ahead of Thursday's staff meeting I wanted to flag a few items I'd like to add to the agenda:

1. Scheduling coverage: I have a conference Nov 6–7 and want to coordinate coverage with Marcus.
2. EHR update: The new intake template goes live Nov 1 — staff will need a 30-minute walkthrough before then.
3. Supervision hours log: Can we set a standing 15-minute slot each week to review?
4. Holiday office hours: Propose we close Nov 27–28 and Dec 24–26. Wanted team alignment before posting.

Also — do we have a confirmed start time? I'll be coming from NSU and need to block travel time.

Thanks,
Rachel

subject: Re: Oct 22 staff meeting — agenda items
snippet: Rachel — great items, adding all four to the agenda. Meeting starts at 10 AM at the office...
body:
Rachel,

Great items — all four are going on the agenda. Meeting is Thursday Oct 22 at 10:00 AM at the office (3300 N Federal Hwy). Should wrap by noon.

Danielle, can you send a calendar hold to Rachel and Marcus with the conference room blocked?

I'll circulate the full agenda by Tuesday EOD. Rachel, plan on leading the EHR walkthrough item — I'll let the vendor know we need someone on standby by phone for questions.

Karen

subject: Speaking inquiry — Q1 2027 availability
snippet: Dr. Bell — we'd love to invite you to speak at our Q1 2027 behavioral health symposium. Reaching out early to check availability...
body:
Dear Dr. Bell,

We'd love to invite you to speak at Baptist Health's Q1 2027 Behavioral Health Symposium in Miami. We're reaching out early to check your availability before we finalize the program.

The symposium is a half-day event for roughly 200 clinicians, and we're particularly interested in a keynote on culturally responsive psychiatric care. Tentative dates are the last week of February or first week of March 2027.

Would you be open to discussing? Happy to share more details and talk through honorarium and travel.

Best regards,
Baptist Health Events Team

subject: Nov 6-7 coverage — confirming I can take refills
snippet: Karen — confirming I can cover urgent med refills while Rachel is at the conference Nov 6-7...
body:
Karen,

Confirming I can cover urgent medication refills while Rachel is at her conference Nov 6-7. I'll be in the office both days and can take same-day adds if needed.

One thing — I have a standing supervision call with a student Friday at 11, so if you can route anything urgent before or after that window it would help.

Thanks,
Marcus

subject: Recredentialing packet received — Bell Behavioral Health
snippet: We have received your recredentialing application. No further action is needed at this time...
body:
Dear Provider,

We have received the recredentialing application for Bell Behavioral Health. Your submission is complete and no further action is needed at this time.

Processing typically takes 60-90 days. You will receive written confirmation once the review is finalized. Your current panel participation remains active throughout the review period.

If you have questions, contact Provider Services at the number on your contract.

Florida Blue Provider Services

subject: November coalition agenda — great, send the brief
snippet: Karen — yes, please add the crisis services item and send the one-pager ahead of time...
body:
Karen,

Yes, please add the crisis services expansion item to the November agenda — it's exactly the kind of thing the coalition should be tackling. A one-page brief ahead of the meeting would be perfect; it'll help the newer members come prepared.

If you can include the referral data broken out by zip code, that would strengthen the case considerably. Looking forward to it.

Lydia

subject: Re: Claire's birthday dinner — Nov 15 plans
snippet: Steak 954 sounds great. I can leave Boca around 4 Saturday and crash at the house...
body:
Steak 954 sounds great. I can leave Boca around 4 on Saturday and crash at the house that night. I'll text when I'm leaving.

Don't tell Claire I'm coming — let's make that part of the surprise. Should I pick anything up on the way down? Mom, want me to grab the cake so you don't have to make the trip Saturday afternoon?

Oliver

subject: SharkLink: Fall 2026 midterm evaluations now open
snippet: The Fall 2026 midterm evaluation window is now open in the faculty portal. Submissions are due October 15...
body:
This is an automated notice from the NSU SharkLink Faculty Portal.

The Fall 2026 midterm evaluation window is now open. Faculty teaching graduate courses must submit narrative evaluations for each enrolled student by October 15, 2026.

To begin, log in to SharkLink and navigate to Faculty Services > Evaluations. For technical assistance, contact the IT Help Desk at nova-it-help@nova.edu.

Do not reply to this message.

Provider: Dr. Susan Pearson, Endocrinology
Location: Cleveland Clinic Florida, Weston
Please arrive 15 minutes early and bring a current medication list.

If you need to reschedule, call the office or use the MyChart portal. Lab work, if ordered, should be completed before your visit.

Thank you,
Cleveland Clinic Florida

subject: Patient portal updated for holiday closures
snippet: Karen — I've updated the portal and the voicemail greeting with the holiday closures once you confirmed...
body:
Hi Karen,

I've updated the patient portal banner and the after-hours voicemail greeting to reflect the holiday closures: closed Nov 27-28 and Dec 24-26. The on-call line will forward to your cell for emergencies during the December closure as we discussed.

I also added a note that medication refill requests should be submitted at least 72 hours before the closures. Let me know if you'd like any wording changed.

Danielle

subject: Thank you — Dec 5 panel confirmation and W-9 received
snippet: Karen — we received your W-9 and confirmed your room at the Loews for Dec 4. Everything is set for the panel...
body:
Karen,

Thank you — we received your W-9 and have confirmed your room at the Loews Miami Beach for the night of December 4. Everything is set for your 2:15-2:35 PM segment on the December 5 panel.

We'll pre-load your slides on the house laptop as requested; please send the final PPTX by November 28. A run-of-show and parking instructions will follow the week before.

We're delighted to have you. Thank you for supporting NAMI Florida.

NAMI Florida Events

subject: EHR intake template walkthrough — proposed times
snippet: Karen — for the Nov 1 intake template go-live, I can run the staff walkthrough on Oct 29 or Oct 30...
body:

For the November 1 intake template go-live, I can run the 30-minute staff walkthrough on either October 29 or October 30, mid-morning. I'd suggest the 29th so we have a buffer day before the template goes live.

I'll prepare a one-page quick-reference for the front desk and providers. Could you confirm the vendor will have someone available by phone that day in case questions come up?

Thanks,
Rachel

subject: Asheville cabin — found availability
snippet: Looked at the cabin near Black Mountain — it's open Dec 27 through 30. Want me to book it?...
body:
Karen,

I looked at the cabin near Black Mountain — it's open December 27 through 30, which gets Claire back before debate resumes. Same place with the wraparound porch and the fireplace we liked.

Want me to go ahead and book it? Oliver said he's in. We can do the Biltmore lights one evening and hit that bookshop in Hendersonville on the way back.

John

Hope the semester is going well. Just a reminder that midterm evaluations for your section of Cultural Competence in Psychiatric Practice (PMHNP 612) are due by October 15 in the NSU faculty portal.

Please complete a brief narrative eval for each enrolled student covering: engagement and participation, clinical application of course concepts, and any areas for development.

Best,
Nadine Cherry, PhD
Adjunct Faculty Coordinator, NSU PMHNP Program

body:
Nadine,

Thank you for the reminder. I have portal access and will have all evaluations submitted by October 14 — one day ahead of the deadline.

Quick question: for students who have missed more than one class session, should I note attendance in the narrative or flag it separately through the dean's office process?

Karen

subject: Q3 QuickBooks reconciliation — items for your review
snippet: Karen — completed the Q3 reconciliation in QuickBooks. Three items need your sign-off before I close the quarter...
body:
Karen,

Completed the Q3 reconciliation in QuickBooks. Overall the quarter looks clean. Three items need your attention before I close the period:

1. Two credit card charges in September ($340 total) are categorized as "Meals & Entertainment" but the receipts haven't been uploaded. Can you locate and upload those?
2. The continuing education reimbursement for Dr. Foster — I need confirmation this should be booked as a payroll benefit vs. a direct expense.
3. The deposit from the NSU adjunct stipend (Q3 installment) needs to be confirmed as personal vs. practice income for allocation purposes.

Please respond by Oct 20 so I can close Q3 and begin the Q4 setup.

Anthony Beaumont, CPA

subject: Claire's birthday dinner — Nov 15 plans
snippet: Hey — have you thought about where you want to take Claire for her birthday dinner? She mentioned Louie Bossi or Steak 954...
body:
Hey Karen,

Have you thought about where you want to take Claire for her birthday dinner on the 15th? She dropped a hint last week — mentioned Louie Bossi or Steak 954. Oliver said he can drive down from Boca that Saturday and would be back Sunday.

Should we make it a surprise dinner or let her pick? And do you want to see if Philip can drive over from Orlando, or keep it just the four of us this year?

Also — cake from Rosarians or do you want to order from that bakery on Federal again?

John

subject: Re: Claire's birthday dinner — Nov 15 plans
snippet: Let's do Steak 954 — Claire has been talking about it since summer. I'll make a reservation for 7 PM...
body:
Let's do Steak 954 — Claire has been talking about it since the summer. I'll call for a 7 PM reservation this week and put it under our name. Oliver, glad you can make it — just text me your ETA Saturday afternoon.

Let's keep it a surprise and tell her we're just going to dinner. Keep it immediate family only this year — I'll ask Philip if he wants to drive down Sunday for the dessert get-together at the house.

I'll order the cake from the bakery on Federal (the salted caramel one she loved last year). Will pick it up Saturday afternoon.

Karen

subject: Oct 22 staff meeting — agenda items
snippet: Hi Karen — ahead of Thursday's staff meeting I wanted to flag a few items I'd like to add to the agenda...
body:
Hi Karen,

Ahead of Thursday's staff meeting I wanted to flag a few items I'd like to add to the agenda:

1. Scheduling coverage: I have a conference Nov 6–7 and want to coordinate coverage with Marcus.
2. EHR update: The new intake template goes live Nov 1 — staff will need a 30-minute walkthrough before then.
3. Supervision hours log: Can we set a standing 15-minute slot each week to review?
4. Holiday office hours: Propose we close Nov 27–28 and Dec 24–26. Wanted team alignment before posting.

Also — do we have a confirmed start time? I'll be coming from NSU and need to block travel time.

Thanks,
Rachel

subject: Re: Oct 22 staff meeting — agenda items
snippet: Rachel — great items, adding all four to the agenda. Meeting starts at 10 AM at the office...
body:
Rachel,

Great items — all four are going on the agenda. Meeting is Thursday Oct 22 at 10:00 AM at the office (3300 N Federal Hwy). Should wrap by noon.

Danielle, can you send a calendar hold to Rachel and Marcus with the conference room blocked?

I'll circulate the full agenda by Tuesday EOD. Rachel, plan on leading the EHR walkthrough item — I'll let the vendor know we need someone on standby by phone for questions.

Karen

subject: Speaking inquiry — Q1 2027 availability
snippet: Dr. Bell — we'd love to invite you to speak at our Q1 2027 behavioral health symposium. Reaching out early to check availability...
body:
Dear Dr. Bell,

We'd love to invite you to speak at Baptist Health's Q1 2027 Behavioral Health Symposium in Miami. We're reaching out early to check your availability before we finalize the program.

The symposium is a half-day event for roughly 200 clinicians, and we're particularly interested in a keynote on culturally responsive psychiatric care. Tentative dates are the last week of February or first week of March 2027.

Would you be open to discussing? Happy to share more details and talk through honorarium and travel.

Best regards,
Baptist Health Events Team

subject: Nov 6-7 coverage — confirming I can take refills
snippet: Karen — confirming I can cover urgent med refills while Rachel is at the conference Nov 6-7...
body:
Karen,

Confirming I can cover urgent medication refills while Rachel is at her conference Nov 6-7. I'll be in the office both days and can take same-day adds if needed.

One thing — I have a standing supervision call with a student Friday at 11, so if you can route anything urgent before or after that window it would help.

Thanks,
Marcus

subject: Recredentialing packet received — Bell Behavioral Health

body:
Dear Provider,

We have received the recredentialing application for Bell Behavioral Health. Your submission is complete and no further action is needed at this time.

Processing typically takes 60-90 days. You will receive written confirmation once the review is finalized. Your current panel participation remains active throughout the review period.

If you have questions, contact Provider Services at the number on your contract.

Florida Blue Provider Services

subject: November coalition agenda — great, send the brief
snippet: Karen — yes, please add the crisis services item and send the one-pager ahead of time...
body:
Karen,

Yes, please add the crisis services expansion item to the November agenda — it's exactly the kind of thing the coalition should be tackling. A one-page brief ahead of the meeting would be perfect; it'll help the newer members come prepared.

If you can include the referral data broken out by zip code, that would strengthen the case considerably. Looking forward to it.

Lydia

subject: Re: Claire's birthday dinner — Nov 15 plans
snippet: Steak 954 sounds great. I can leave Boca around 4 Saturday and crash at the house...
body:
Steak 954 sounds great. I can leave Boca around 4 on Saturday and crash at the house that night. I'll text when I'm leaving.

Don't tell Claire I'm coming — let's make that part of the surprise. Should I pick anything up on the way down? Mom, want me to grab the cake so you don't have to make the trip Saturday afternoon?

Oliver

John

subject: Lease renewal packet — review ahead of October renewal
snippet: Hi Karen — the Coral Ridge Professional Center lease renewal packet arrived early. The renewal term begins October 1...
body:
Hi Karen,

The lease renewal packet from Coral Ridge Professional Center arrived today. The renewal term begins October 1; the landlord wants signed intent well ahead of that date or we roll month-to-month at a 12% premium, so they sent it early this year.

Danielle

Danielle, go ahead and leave the packet on my desk. I'll review this week, and we'll target signing well ahead of the October renewal.

Karen

NAMI Florida Events

subject: Speaking inquiry — Q1 2027 availability
snippet: Dr. Bell — we'd love to invite you to speak at our Q1 2027 behavioral health symposium. Reaching out early to check availability...
body:
Dear Dr. Bell,

We'd love to invite you to speak at Baptist Health's Q1 2027 Behavioral Health Symposium in Miami. We're reaching out early to check your availability before we finalize the program.

The symposium is a half-day event for roughly 200 clinicians, and we're particularly interested in a keynote on culturally responsive psychiatric care. Tentative dates are the last week of February or first week of March 2027.

Would you be open to discussing? Happy to share more details and talk through honorarium and travel.

Best regards,
Baptist Health Events Team

subject: Lease renewal packet — review ahead of October renewal
snippet: Hi Karen — the Coral Ridge Professional Center lease renewal packet arrived early. The renewal term begins October 1...
body:
Hi Karen,

The lease renewal packet from Coral Ridge Professional Center arrived today. The renewal term begins October 1; the landlord wants signed intent well ahead of that date or we roll month-to-month at a 12% premium, so they sent it early this year.

I've flagged three items for your review:
1. Rent escalation clause (Sec. 4.2) — 3% annual, same as current
2. CAM cap increased from 8% to 10%
3. Option to renew for an additional 3 years added at our request

I can drop the packet on your desk this afternoon. Should I also loop in Anthony Beaumont to review the financial terms before you sign?

Danielle

subject: Re: Lease renewal packet — review ahead of October renewal
snippet: Danielle — yes, please loop in Anthony. I'll review this week...
body:
Danielle,

Thank you for flagging this promptly. Yes, please loop in Anthony — copying him here. Anthony, can you review Sec. 4.2 and the CAM cap change and let me know if there are any concerns by the end of next week?

Danielle, go ahead and leave the packet on my desk. I'll review this week, and we'll target signing well ahead of the October renewal.

Karen

subject: Dec 5 NAMI FL Panel — logistics and AV needs
snippet: Karen — you're confirmed for the Dec 5 panel. A few logistics details before we send the final confirmation...
body:
Karen,

You're confirmed for the December 5 NAMI Florida panel at the Miami Beach Convention Center. A few logistics items before we send the final confirmation to the venue:

1. Panel title: "Mental Health Equity in South Florida: Gaps, Gains, and the Road Ahead" — still works for you?
2. Your 20-minute segment is slotted 2:15–2:35 PM. Full panel Q&A runs 2:35–3:15 PM.
3. AV: Do you need a clicker/laser pointer or will slides be pre-loaded on the house laptop?
4. Hotel block at the Loews Miami Beach closes Nov 14. Would you like a room reserved on the event account?
5. Honorarium paperwork — please send your W-9 to this address at your convenience.

Let us know on the above and we'll get everything finalized.

NAMI Florida Events

snippet: Panel title works perfectly. Here are my answers to your logistics questions...
body:
Thank you. Panel title works perfectly. Here are my answers:

1. Title confirmed.
2. 2:15–2:35 PM slot confirmed — I'll plan to be on-site by 1:30 PM.
3. Please pre-load slides on the house laptop. I'll send the deck (16:9 PPTX) by Nov 28. I do have my own clicker if needed.
4. Yes, please reserve a room on the event account for the night of Dec 4. One king, non-smoking.
5. W-9 attached — let me know if you need anything else from me.

Looking forward to it.

Karen Bell, PMHNP-BC
Bell Behavioral Health
(954) 555-0100

Hope the semester is going well. Just a reminder that midterm evaluations for your section of Cultural Competence in Psychiatric Practice (PMHNP 612) are due by October 15 in the NSU faculty portal.

Anthony Beaumont, CPA
